answer:
Sentry's REST API uses Bearer token authentication [1][2]. You must include an Authorization header with your token in every request [3][1]. DSNs are not supported for the REST API and are used exclusively by SDKs for event ingestion [1][2].

Example request:

```bash
curl https://sentry.io/api/0/organizations/ \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN'
```

You can use personal tokens (tied to your user account) or organization tokens (tied to an organization) [1][2]. Tokens can be created via User Settings or Organization Settings, respectively [3][1][2]. For third-party applications, Sentry also supports OAuth2 [3].

results.title: Retrieve an Event for a Project
